title: Java Time Formatting
description: Practice zones, instants, periods, and date-time formatting.
learningGoals: Recall that Instant is a timeline point and ZoneId gives it local calendar meaning.
Recognize that Period is date-based, unlike time-based durations.

completionChecks.prompt: What does this code print for an instant of 2026-06-08T10:15:30Z?
completionChecks.explanation: Europe/Stockholm is UTC+2 in June (summer time), so the instant shows as 12:15 local; adding a one-month Period gives 2026-07-08, formatted as "2026-07-08 12:15".

choices: A clock-based duration measured in seconds
A date-based amount of one calendar month
completionChecks.explanation: Period is date-based, so one month spans a variable number of days depending on the calendar, unlike a time-based Duration measured in seconds.

completionChecks.prompt: What does local.getHour() return immediately after now.atZone(zone)?
completionChecks.explanation: Europe/Stockholm is two hours ahead of UTC for this June instant, so 10:15Z becomes 12:15 locally.
